methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Oxford Quantum Circuits is a Series B quantum computing company founded by Peter Leek that has raised over $100M to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 comparable companies: 3 public (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private peers including Q-CTRL, Quantinuum, and IonQ.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable valuation method given the dataset constraints. Using the $100M total raised as a proxy for entry valuation and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $190M. The peer-median method proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the quantum computing sector; although 7 same-sector companies exist, fewer than three qualify under narrow stage and tier filters, making a cross-sector median unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $162M to $218M, with a midpoint of $190M. This range reflects a ±15% sensitivity band around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the absence of recent same-stage transaction data in quantum computing, limiting calibration of the step-up multiplier, and the heterogeneity of quantum approaches across the peer set, which constrains direct comparability. The single-method reliance also constrains confidence; validation against revenue or technology-stage metrics would strengthen the estimate.
